title: Path Traversal
principle: Never join user input straight into a filesystem path. Resolve the combined path to its canonical absolute form and verify it still sits inside the intended base directory before opening it; reject anything that escapes with ../ or an absolute path. Better still, don't accept a path at all — map an opaque id to a known file.

snippets.secure:
target = os.path.realpath(os.path.join(BASE, name))
if not target.startswith(os.path.realpath(BASE) + os.sep):
    abort(400)
open(target)
snippets.note: realpath resolves .. and symlinks; the startswith check confines it to BASE. os.path.join alone does not stop ../ or an absolute name.
path.resolve normalizes .. ; the prefix check keeps it under BASE. path.join by itself will happily produce ../../etc/passwd.

snippets.library: Path.normalize + startsWith on the resolved base
snippets.note: normalize() collapses ../ ; startsWith(base) confines it. Use toRealPath on the base to also resolve symlinks.
filepath.Join calls Clean, collapsing ../, but you still must confirm the result stays under base. Resolve symlinks with filepath.EvalSymlinks for stronger guarantees.
